cons: DSG7 DQ200 issues on auto versions
knownFaults: 1.2 TSI (CBZA/CBZB): timing chain stretch — listen for rattle on cold start, CRITICAL. DSG7 DQ200: jerking/hesitation — software update partial fix only. 1.4 TDI: timing belt every 120,000km. Oil leaks on TSI engines.
notes: Best budget city car in Slovakia. Avoid DSG auto if possible. 1.0 TSI post-2017 is most reliable engine.

pros: Largest boot in small car class (380L)
Modern 5-star NCAP with proper safety tech
cons: 1.0 TSI timing chain check on early units
DSG7 DQ200 same concerns as Mk3
knownFaults: 1.0 TSI 95/110hp pre-2022: timing chain tensioner. DSG7 DQ200: low-speed jerk. Infotainment reboot on 2021 production.
notes: Significant improvement over Mk3. Sweet spot for reliability vs price.

pros: #1 most reliable family car — 99.3% What Car? 2024
cons: DSG7 DQ200 on 1.2/1.4 TSI: clutch pack wear 60,000-95,000km
1.2 TSI timing chain on early examples
knownFaults: DSG7 DQ200: clutch pack wear — budget €1,400-2,500 repair. 1.4 TSI: water pump failure ~100,000km. EGR valve fouling on TDI after 150,000km. Airbag inflator recall 2012-2014. Child lock recall 2016-2017.
notes: BEST VALUE in database. 99.3% reliability. Mk3 Octavia 1.6 TDI manual with full history = best used car purchase under €15,000.

pros: 610L boot — best value family estate in Europe
1,700kg towing — pulls trailer or small caravan
Dogs, bikes, kids, holidays — handles everything
cons: Slightly higher price than hatchback same year
Same DSG concerns on auto versions
knownFaults: Identical to hatchback. Additional: boot water ingress — check spare wheel well for dampness. Tailgate seal condition.
notes: Definitive Slovak family car. 1.6 TDI Combi manual full history under €15,000 = arguably best used car in country.

reliability: Below Average — What Car? 24th/26 family cars
cons: CRITICAL: Mk4 dropped to 24th/26 family cars — 54% of cars reported faults
Third of repairs took over a week
knownFaults: Infotainment crashes/freezes/black screens (31% of faults). Water pump failures. Underbonnet cover recall 2020-2022. Brake servo heat shield recall on DSG. Damper premature wear on poor roads.
notes: If reliability is priority — buy Mk3. If modern tech matters — buy Mk4 but verify ALL recalls first. Gap between generations is real and significant.

cons: Higher price than Mk3 for worse reliability
knownFaults: Identical to Mk4 hatchback. Additional: boot water ingress potential.
notes: Best modern family estate if you verify all recalls. For pure reliability — find a late Mk3 Combi instead.

pros: Business car feel for little money
cons: Trunk lid wiring harness fraying — known issue
knownFaults: CRITICAL: trunk lid wiring harness frays — causes lock/electrical failure. Pre-2012 CDAA V6: piston ring oil consumption. 4x4 rear diff pump — change oil every 60,000km. DSG6 mechatronic on high mileage.
notes: Budget luxury. Great daily if mechanically sorted. Not for family — pedestrian safety too low. Manager who wants space without spending much.

knownFaults: All B6 liftback issues. Tailgate strut failure common. Rear suspension bushes wear.
notes: Unbeatable budget estate. €7,000 Superb Combi 2.0 TDI with full service history = outstanding value buy.

pros: Near-BMW interior quality at Škoda price
2.0 TDI 150hp DSG6: sweet spot reliability + efficiency
cons: High trims push price into BMW territory
knownFaults: 1.4 TSI: intake valve carbon buildup (GDI). DSG7 DQ200 on 1.4 TSI: low-speed jerk. 2.0 TDI EGR fouling 100,000km+. Prefer DSG6 on TDI — much more reliable.
notes: SWEET SPOT: B8 Superb 2.0 TDI 150hp DSG6 = best value business car on used market. Manager's car feeling at fraction of BMW cost.

pros: 660L boot — among largest estates in Europe
knownFaults: Same as liftback. Tailgate struts wear. Water via tailgate washer tube some units.
notes: Best large estate under €25,000. Perfect: business refinement + family practicality + real towing. B8 Combi 2.0 TDI 4x4 is one of best all-round used cars available.

knownFaults: Insufficient data.
notes: For buyers wanting the latest. Buy B8 if reliability data matters more than tech.

knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Most practical estate at price. Beats Passat on space, beats BMW on price. Best for someone wanting everything in one car.

knownFaults: CRITICAL: 1.6 TDI EA189 Dieselgate recall — verify by VIN. 1.4 TSI: timing chain and coil packs. DSG7 DQ200 issues. Water pump and thermostat on high mileage.
notes: Budget only. Too old and too many known issues for family use.

cons: Reliability slipped from 18th to 73rd in Driver Power as it aged
DQ200 DSG7 issues on lower engines
Timing chains on petrols — full history essential
knownFaults: DQ200 DSG7: clutch pack and mechatronic — verify software updates. Water pump typically 80,000-120,000km. Timing chain 1.2/1.4 TSI pre-2016 — cold start rattle. 1.6 TDI EGR. 2.0 TDI AdBlue 2015+.
notes: Better than Mk8, worse than Octavia Mk3. Mk7.5 (2017-2020) is pick. Avoid early Mk7 2013-2014 1.2 TSI automatics.

reliability: Below Average — significant early software issues
cons: 2020-2021: widespread infotainment crashes, climate control faults, screen failures
More expensive than Octavia for less space
knownFaults: CRITICAL 2020-2021: infotainment complete crashes, instrument cluster software failures, touch panel malfunctions. Many OTA updates issued. 1.5 eTSI timing chain. BISG starter-generator check.
notes: Buy 2022+ build date only. Early Mk8 not recommended. Better alternative: buy an Octavia Mk3 instead.

pros: E91 Touring: practical estate with driving pleasure
cons: Age TUV defect rate rising significantly
FRM footwell module failure — cripples multiple systems
Electric steering rack failure E90 specific
knownFaults: E90/E91 SPECIFIC: FRM footwell module failure — affects lights/wipers/locking, €400-800. Electric power steering rack failure — sudden loss, €1,500+. Water pump and thermostat housing plastic — fails ~100,000km. N47 320d diesel: timing chain on REAR of engine — engine-out to repl
notes: Best-driving budget car. High reward high risk. N47 diesel timing chain is single biggest risk — avoid or price in. 320i petrol manual with full history is safest E90.

pros: ZF 8-speed automatic — one of best gearboxes ever
cons: EGR cooler leak on diesel F30/F31 2015-2017 recall
knownFaults: N20 engine (320i, 328i 2012-2015): timing chain guide failure — CRITICAL. Rattle on cold start or acceleration = imminent failure. Budget €2,000-3,000. EGR cooler recall 2015-2017 diesel — verify. Power steering recall pre-Dec 2015 F30 — verify. B48 petrol post-2015: oil dilution
notes: Sweet spot: F30 320d post-2015 ZF auto full service history. AVOID N20 petrol — timing chain risk too high. 340i B58 engine is very reliable. F31 Touring 320d = outstanding estate.

pros: G21 Touring: 500L boot, perfect business estate
knownFaults: B48 petrol: oil dilution — check oil quality/smell. B47 diesel: EGR cooler ~150,000km. Cooling system plastic parts. Suspension wear on Slovak roads.
notes: Best company car if budget allows running costs. 320d Touring G21 = definitive business estate. Most reliable 3 Series generation yet.

knownFaults: Very few. 1.3 petrol: occasional oil consumption on high mileage. Hybrid battery check if over 150,000km.
notes: Bulletproof city car. Reliable budget choice.

pros: 84% pedestrian safety — massive improvement vs Mk3
3.8L/100km hybrid: cheapest to run in class
knownFaults: Virtually none. Minor Bluetooth 2020 early units — software fix.
notes: Best city car for reliability and running costs. Hybrid pays for premium within 2-3 years.

knownFaults: 2.5 D-4D: EGR fouling after 150,000km. DPF blocking short trips (post-2009). Injector issues 3.0 D-4D. Underbody rust on older examples. Timing belt — check history.
notes: Budget workhorse. Pre-DPF versions (pre-2009) often preferred for hard off-road use. Nothing cheaper for this level of durability.

knownFaults: 2.4 D-4D: EGR fouling on city use — needs motorway runs. DPF blocking short trips. 2.8 D-4D: timing chain stretch on very high mileage. Injector check all engines.
notes: Reference truck. 3,500kg towing, 278mm clearance, proven. Best for anyone who genuinely works their truck.

cons: 2.2 TDCi EGR and DPF issues well documented
knownFaults: 2.2 TDCi: EGR valve and DPF issues very common. Timing belt — check change history. 3.2 TDCi: expensive failures — injectors and fuel system. 2.0 EcoBlue 2019+: early injector concerns.
notes: Good budget truck. Better dealer network than Hilux in some areas. Not as legendarily durable but significantly cheaper used.

knownFaults: New gen insufficient data. Watch 2.0 EcoBlue injectors — Transit siblings have documented issues.
notes: Best comfort/technology in pickup class. Better daily driver than Hilux. Not as proven for extreme durability.

knownFaults: 7DCT dual clutch: hesitation/judder low speed. 1.6 GDI: carbon buildup intake valves. 2.0 CRDi: DPF issues city use.
notes: Great budget SUV. Check 7-year warranty still applies — massive advantage. 2.0 CRDi 4x4 manual most reliable choice.

knownFaults: DCT low-speed judder — check software update. 1.6 GDI carbon buildup same concern.
notes: 7-year warranty game-changing. If under 7 years old warranty covers major repairs. Excellent all-rounder.

pros: Extremely cheap to buy and run
knownFaults: 1.5 dCi: EGR and DPF issues. Underbody rust — check thoroughly. Suspension bushes wear. 4x4 all modes test.
notes: Only if budget is critical constraint. Not for family. Good for farm/rural light off-road only.

pros: Best value AWD SUV in Europe
cons: 3-star NCAP still — 61% adult protection below modern standards
knownFaults: CVT overheating heavy use/towing. 1.5 dCi DPF issues. 1.0 TCe some oil consumption. LPG certification check.
notes: Significantly better than 1st gen. Still low safety. Best for farm/rural/outdoor where off-road matters more than crash safety. Never primary family car.

pros: Best value AWD in Europe at price
cons: No Euro NCAP yet — unknown safety
knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Best value entry SUV if NCAP confirms safety improvement. Await results.

knownFaults: Virtually none. CVT noise — character not fault. Hybrid battery check on very high mileage.
notes: Boring but brilliant. Best reliability at this price range.

pros: 4.5L/100km real-world cheapest to run in class
knownFaults: Extremely few. Early-unit minor infotainment quirks resolved by software.
notes: Best reliability in class. 96% adult NCAP remarkable. Touring Sports 2.0 hybrid = outstanding long-distance family car.

reliability: Good — What Car? 95.5% large SUV class
pros: Only 7% faults in survey — low rate
knownFaults: 7DCT: hesitation/judder — software update helps. Clutch/flywheel: excessive wear manual versions — check pedal feel. Window regulators 2013-2016. 2.0 CRDi DPF city use.
notes: Solid. 5-year warranty advantage. 2.0 CRDi manual 4x4 most reliable drivetrain. Avoid 2015 — worst year.

knownFaults: 2022 early production: engine stalling/misfires reported. Windshield cracking from edges — check. DCT hesitation. Tow hitch wiring harness recall.
notes: Best boot (620L). 5-year warranty covers issues. Avoid very early 2022 — post-mid-2022 build preferred.

pros: Best camper conversion base — huge ecosystem
cons: Dual mass flywheel failure — VERY common
knownFaults: Dual mass flywheel: rattle/judder on clutch — budget €800-1,200 replacement. EGR failure — limp mode, engine light. Timing belt: every 120,000km — check stamps. DSG mechatronic on auto.
notes: Camper conversion benchmark. Flywheel and EGR are expected costs — price them in. 2010-2015 facelift preferred.

knownFaults: DSG mechatronic — €1,500-2,500. DPF short-trip blocking. Timing belt every 180,000km some say earlier — check. Rear window water ingress.
notes: T6.1 (2019 facelift) preferred. Extraordinary resale value. Best camper conversion base currently available.

reliability: Good — 2nd in large SUV What Car? 2024
pros: Safer than BMW X3, Audi Q5
cons: AEB recall: 736,430 cars — verify software update
Petrol: broken piston reports on some units
knownFaults: AEB recall: emergency brake may not engage — 736,430 cars affected, software update required, verify VIN. Petrol 2.0T: broken pistons/crank seal leaks some units. Panoramic sunroof drain blockage causes water. DPF diesel short trips. Seatbelt recall 2021.
notes: Outstanding safety, better reliability than German rivals at same price. AEB recall must be verified. Best alternative to BMW X3 — more safety, competitive price.

pros: 98% adult NCAP — best estate safety in class
cons: AEB recall same as XC60 — verify
knownFaults: AEB recall — same as XC60, software update verify. SRS 2020 recall. Low-pressure fuel pump fuse some 2019-2020. Infotainment early units: screen lag.
notes: 98% adult NCAP — safest estate available. Reliability improved but below Toyota/Honda. Best for safety-first buyers wanting premium estate.

knownFaults: 2.2D: DPF short city trips — needs motorway runs. Minor infotainment bugs. Auto hesitation high mileage.
notes: Underrated. Excellent value 1st gen. 2.2D AWD manual: outstanding economy and reliability.

knownFaults: 2.2D DPF short trips. Auto occasional hesitation. Otherwise excellent.
notes: Most reliable SUV in class after RAV4. 3.1% TUV rivals Japanese cars. Best: reliability AND driving quality together.

cons: Diesel injector issues some early units
knownFaults: 1.6 i-DTEC: timing belt check (every 200,000km). Diesel injector issues early 2013. CVT: rough high mileage. Oil leaks diesel.
notes: Undervalued. Honda reliability plus 589L boot at bargain price. 1.6 i-DTEC AWD manual: outstanding combination.

cons: CRITICAL 1.5T petrol: oil dilution with fuel in cold climates on early production
knownFaults: CRITICAL: 1.5 VTEC Turbo 2018-2019 cold climate: fuel diluting engine oil. Check oil level and smell. Honda issued software updates. Hybrid: very reliable — no issue.
notes: Strongly prefer hybrid over 1.5T petrol in cold Slovak climate. Oil dilution issue very relevant here. Hybrid avoids entirely.

knownFaults: EDC dual clutch: jerking/hesitation — software helps but not always fixes. 0.9 TCe: oil consumption, timing chain. 1.5 dCi: EGR, DPF.
notes: Budget city only. Manual manual much more reliable than EDC. Urban short trips only.

cons: Hybrid: electrical glitches noted — dealer visits
knownFaults: Hybrid: electrical issues requiring dealer visits. EDC improved but check. 1.0 TCe timing chain reports.
notes: Improved vs 4th gen. Better than 4th gen but below Yaris reliability. Electric hybrid avoids EDC issue — hybrid manual not available.

pros: e-208 electric made in Trnava Slovakia
cons: CRITICAL: 1.2 PureTech timing chain failure — class action filed France
knownFaults: CRITICAL: 1.2 PureTech timing chain stretch — catastrophic failure possible. Class action filed in France. Listen for ANY cold start rattle. Full service history with regular oil changes mandatory — missed services accelerate chain wear.
notes: Beautiful car. Serious engine flaw. Diesel version avoids problem. Electric version best option. Buy petrol ONLY with perfect unbroken service history.

knownFaults: SAME: 1.2 PureTech timing chain CRITICAL. Infotainment i-Cockpit screen freeze some units.
notes: Same engine risk as 208. Diesel version avoids timing chain issue. Electric version cleanest choice.

reliability: Average — SEAT in TUV lower positions consistently
pros: Sporty looks — better looking than Golf
cons: TUV worse than VW despite same platform
knownFaults: Identical to Golf Mk7 — same VW platform. DSG7 DQ200. 1.4 TSI timing chain. EGR TDI. SEAT scores worse in TUV despite identical mechanicals.
notes: Cheaper Golf. Good value if you accept slightly worse reliability data. Leon ST estate: excellent practical value.

knownFaults: PHEV: engine overheating/coolant issue — recall issued, verify completed. 1.5 EcoBoost: coolant loss cylinder head gasket on some. Powershift DCT: shudder. 2.0 TDCi: EGR/DPF.
notes: Avoid PHEV unless recall confirmed complete. 2.0 TDCi AWD manual most reliable drivetrain.

knownFaults: S-Tronic DCT on 1.4/2.0 TFSI: hesitation/jerking. 2.0 TDI: DPF/EGR 150,000km+. 1.4 TFSI oil consumption, timing chain. Virtual cockpit software glitch.
notes: Best materials quality. Reliability slightly below BMW. 2.0 TDI Quattro Avant S-Tronic: definitive specification. Avant one of best-looking estates available.
